app.empty_state_no_chats: No chats yet — create one on the left to get started.
app.non_local_host.title: Ollama host is not local
app.title: FlameChat {version}
attach.empty: The file “{name}” is empty (0 bytes). There is nothing to analyse or describe. Check your program's export and pick the file again.
attach.missing:
The file “{name}” doesn't seem to exist any more.
It may have been moved or deleted since you picked it. Please pick the file again.
attach.not_a_file: “{name}” is not a file — it's a folder or a symlink. Please pick a single file.
attach.text_too_big:
The text file “{name}” is {kb:.0f} KB — more than a local model's context window can sensibly hold (limit: {cap:.0f} KB).

Split it into smaller chunks and attach the part that's relevant to you, or summarise the contents with another tool first.
attach.too_big:
The file “{name}” is {mb:.1f} MB, above the {cap:.0f} MB limit.

The limit exists so the model and your machine can still process the file sensibly. Suggestions:
 • Trim the audio to the relevant passage.
 • Export images at a lower resolution.
 • Convert audio to a lossy format like MP3 or Opus.
chat.attach_dialog_text: Pick text or source files (up to 3)
chat.attach_too_many:
You picked {count} files. FlameChat handles at most {limit} per action so the model can still respond sensibly.

Pick a smaller batch and attach the rest in a second step.
chat.attach_wrong_type_audio: The selected file doesn't look like an audio file. FlameChat expects WAV, MP3, M4A, AAC, FLAC, OGG, Opus or AIFF. Convert the file (for instance with Audacity) or pick an audio file directly.
chat.attach_wrong_type_image: The selected file doesn't look like an image. FlameChat recognises images by the extensions PNG, JPG, JPEG, GIF, BMP, WebP, HEIC and TIFF. Pick one of those, or use the “Audio” action instead if you meant to analyse sound.
chat.attach_wrong_type_text: One of the selected files is not a text file. FlameChat recognises text by common extensions (.txt, .md, .json, .yaml, source code and so on) and by MIME type. Pick text files only.
chat.attached_text_header: I'm attaching the following file(s) as context — please take them into account for the next answer.
chat.attachment_saved_prompt: The result is {chars} characters long and would overwhelm the chat. Where would you like to save it?
chat.default_image_prompt: Please describe in detail what is visible in the attached image(s).
chat.empty_body_default: (Please take a look at the attached files.)
chat.empty_state: No messages yet. Type your message below and press Enter to send (Shift+Enter for a new line).
chat.error_box_body:
The request to Ollama was cut short before the response finished.

Common causes: Ollama crashed mid-response, your machine ran out of memory for the chosen model, or the connection was interrupted.

Try the following:
 • Right-click the message and pick “Regenerate”.
 • Switch to a smaller model in Preferences.
 • Quit FlameChat and start it again.

Technical details: {err}
chat.input_hint: Ask something or describe your coding task …
chat.input_label: Message (Enter to send, Shift+Enter for a new line)
chat.intent_dialog_body: Pick what you want FlameChat to do with the next file. Each option opens a file picker filtered to the right type.
chat.intent_dialog_title: Attachment — what would you like to do?
chat.no_model_body:
No language model is selected for this chat — either none is installed, or the one you picked earlier is no longer there.

To get one: open Preferences with Cmd+, and pick a recommended model in the “Models” tab. Once the download is done, choose the model in the dropdown at the top of the chat window.
chat.no_vision_model_body:
Image description needs a model that can actually read images — none of your installed models qualifies.

How to get one: open Preferences with Cmd+, and, in the “Models” tab, download one of the recommended multimodal models. Good picks are Gemma 4, Gemma 3 (4B and up) or Llava. Once it's downloaded, try the image attachment again.
chat.received: Response received. Ready for the next message.
chat.status_ready_new: New chat — type your first message.
list.confirm_delete_body: Really delete chat “{title}”? This cannot be undone.
models.custom_bad_id_body: That reference doesn't look like a valid Ollama name. Examples that work: “llama3.1:8b”, “qwen2.5-coder:7b”, or a Hugging Face reference like “hf.co/TheBloke/Llama-2-7B-GGUF:Q4_K_M”.
models.custom_bad_name_body: The name you chose doesn't match what Ollama accepts. Allowed characters are lowercase letters, digits, dot, underscore and dash, optionally followed by a colon and a tag — e.g. “my-model” or “my-model:q4”.
models.custom_empty_body: Please enter either an Ollama reference (e.g. “llama3.1:8b”) or a URL to a .gguf file.
models.custom_name_a11y: Local name for the downloaded model
models.custom_name_hint: e.g. my-custom-model — leave empty to auto-derive
models.custom_not_gguf_body: FlameChat can only download .gguf model files via URL. The address you gave doesn't end in .gguf — double-check that you copied the direct download link (on Hugging Face that's “Copy download link” from the file page).
models.custom_note: Can't find the model you want in the list above? Enter either an Ollama reference (e.g. “llama3.1:8b” or “hf.co/TheBloke/Llama-2-7B-GGUF:Q4_K_M”) or a direct download URL to a .gguf file. For a URL, FlameChat downloads the file once from the host you specified and hands it to your local Ollama.
models.hw_no_gpu: GPU: no dedicated GPU detected — inference runs on the CPU
models.ollama_unreachable_body:
FlameChat can't reach Ollama right now to list your installed models.

Most likely the Ollama service isn't running (for example after a sleep/wake cycle, or if someone quit it from its menu bar icon).

Next step: quit and reopen FlameChat — it will bring Ollama back up automatically.

Technical details: {err}
models.pull_error_body:
The download didn't finish.

Common causes: interrupted internet connection, not enough free disk space, or the requested model is temporarily unavailable on Ollama's servers.

Try the following:
 • Check your internet connection and retry the download.
 • Make sure you have enough free disk space.
 • Try a smaller model first as a sanity check.

Technical details: {err}
models.pull_no_selection_body:
This button downloads one of the entries in the “Recommended models” list above. Select a row there first.

If you instead want to use your own reference (e.g. “granite4:3b”) or a direct .gguf URL, use the “Download a custom model” section below.
models.pull_not_found_body:
Ollama doesn't know a model with that reference.

Almost always this is a typo in the name — e.g. “llama3.1:8b” instead of “lama3.1:8b”, or “qwen2.5-coder:7b” instead of “qwen:7b”. Double-check the exact spelling at https://ollama.com/library.

For Hugging Face references the shape must be “hf.co/<user>/<repo>:<tag>” and the tag (e.g. “Q4_K_M”) has to match an uploaded file exactly.

Technical details: {err}
ollama.connection_lost:
Connection to Ollama was lost.

Possible cause: Ollama quit in the meantime (for example after sleep/wake, or if someone closed it from its menu bar icon).

Fix: quit FlameChat and reopen it — Ollama will be started back up automatically.
ollama.install.failed_body:
FlameChat couldn't install Ollama automatically.

Technical details: {err}

How to keep going:
 • Check your internet connection and try again.
 • Or install Ollama by hand from https://ollama.com/download — FlameChat will pick it up at the next launch.
ollama.install.no_mount: hdiutil attach produced no mount point.
ollama.install.not_found_after:
The Ollama install ran, but FlameChat couldn't find it afterwards.

That shouldn't normally happen. Try restarting FlameChat. If the problem persists, install Ollama by hand at https://ollama.com/download.
ollama.loopback.https:
The OLLAMA_HOST environment variable uses the https scheme. Talking to a local Ollama on your own machine needs neither TLS nor does Ollama serve it by default.

Fix: set OLLAMA_HOST to http://127.0.0.1:11434, or unset the variable entirely.
ollama.loopback.no_host: OLLAMA_HOST contains no valid hostname. An address like http://127.0.0.1:11434 is expected. Unset the variable or set it to that exact value.
ollama.loopback.not_loopback:
OLLAMA_HOST points at “{host}”, which resolves to {ip} — a remote machine, not your own.

FlameChat only connects to your local machine (127.0.0.1 or ::1) for privacy. Fix: set OLLAMA_HOST to http://127.0.0.1:11434, or unset the variable entirely.
ollama.loopback.resolve_failed:
The hostname “{host}” in OLLAMA_HOST could not be resolved ({err}).

Fix: set OLLAMA_HOST to http://127.0.0.1:11434, or unset the variable.
ollama.loopback.unknown_scheme:
The OLLAMA_HOST environment variable uses an unknown scheme ({scheme!r}). FlameChat only accepts plain http on a loopback address.

Fix: set OLLAMA_HOST to http://127.0.0.1:11434, or unset the variable entirely.
ollama.progress.waiting: Waiting for Ollama to be ready …
ollama.ready_timeout:
Ollama was started but still isn't answering after {timeout} seconds.

Another program may be holding port 11434, or Ollama may be stuck on its first launch.

Try the following:
 • Quit FlameChat and any other Ollama instances, then start FlameChat again.
 • Run `lsof -iTCP:11434 -sTCP:LISTEN` in a terminal to see which process owns the port.
 • Launch Ollama.app manually from your Applications folder and reopen FlameChat.
ollama.unreachable:
FlameChat couldn't reach Ollama.

Most likely the Ollama service is not running. To bring it back:
 • Quit FlameChat and reopen it — Ollama is started back up at launch.
 • Or open Ollama from your applications list manually.
Technical details: {err}
prefs.about.name: FlameChat
prefs.about.privacy_body: Apart from the one-time Ollama download and user-initiated model downloads, no data ever leaves your machine. The connection to the local Ollama server is validated as a loopback address at startup.
prefs.about.shortcuts_body: Cmd+N: new chat · Cmd+M: models · Cmd+,: preferences · Cmd+1: chat list · Cmd+2: message input · Cmd+3: model chooser · Ctrl+Shift+S: speak status · Ctrl+Cmd+Up/Down: navigate between messages · Alt+1 … Alt+0 … Alt+-: speak the last 11 messages (press twice to copy, thrice to read details) · Enter: send · Shift+Enter: new line · Esc: cancel response · Cmd+C: copy message.
prefs.about.tagline: Accessibility-first, fully local AI chat — for conversations and coding.
prefs.chats.auto_create: Create a new empty chat on first launch and after the last chat is deleted (recommended)
prefs.chats.heading: Chat behaviour
prefs.chats.note: When this option is off, FlameChat shows an empty pane if no chats exist — you then create one manually through “+ New chat”.
prefs.general.heading: General
prefs.general.inline_limit_label: Show inline in chat up to (characters):
prefs.general.inline_limit_note: Transcripts and analyses up to this length appear inline in the chat. Longer results are offered as a text file save instead.
prefs.general.language_note: The language change takes effect the next time FlameChat starts.
prefs.general.num_predict_note: Upper limit on how long a single response may get. Only a denominator for the progress bar — the model stops sooner once it is actually done.
prefs.general.theme_note: FlameChat starts in dark mode by default. The switch takes effect immediately; a few niche controls (the menu bar, for instance) only pick up the new colour at the next launch.
prefs.general.whisper_note: Model used when transcribing audio. Larger sizes are more accurate but slower and hungrier for memory. ‘small' is a good balance.
prefs.sounds.heading: Audio feedback
prefs.sounds.note: Note: while the model is writing, a randomly selected typing loop plays in the background. The next run picks a different variant so it does not feel repetitive.
prefs.sounds.typing_toggle: Typing sound while the assistant writes
prefs.title: Preferences
prepare.fail_body:
FlameChat can't start without a working Ollama service, and setup ran into an error.

Details: {err}

What you can try:
 • Launch FlameChat again — a retry often helps, especially after network glitches.
 • Install Ollama manually: https://ollama.com/download, then reopen FlameChat.
 • Make sure you have enough free disk space and a stable internet connection.
prepare.headline: Just a moment — setting up Ollama.
prepare.note: If Ollama is not yet installed, FlameChat fetches the official installer (~150 MB) and sets it up system-wide so the app and your terminal share one install. After that no data leaves your machine unless you explicitly download a language model.
prepare.ollama_waiting: Waiting for Ollama to be ready …
prepare.title: Preparing FlameChat
status.shortcuts: Cmd+1: chat list · Cmd+2: message · Cmd+3: model chooser · Cmd+,: preferences · Ctrl+Shift+S: speak status · Ctrl+Cmd+↑/↓: previous/next message · Alt+1…0…-: speak last 11 messages (twice: copy, thrice: details)
toolbar.connection_tooltip: FlameChat talks to this local Ollama endpoint only. The host was validated as a loopback address at startup.
toolbar.model_tooltip: Pick the model for the active chat (Ctrl+3)
